records.ety: [OE. ministre, F. ministre, fr. L. minister, orig. a double comparative from the root of minor less, and hence meaning, an inferior, a servant. See 1st Minor, and cf. Master, Minstrel.]

defs: A servant; a subordinate; an officer or assistant of inferior rank; hence, an agent, an instrument.
quotes: Moses rose up, and his minister Joshua.
I chose Camillo for the minister, to poison My friend Polixenes.
I cry out the on the ministres, quod he, That shoulde keep and rule this cit.

defs: One to whom the sovereign or executive head of a government intrusts the management of affairs of state, or some department of such affairs.
quotes: Ministers to kings, whose eyes, ears, and hands they are, must be answerable to God and man.

defs: A representative of a government, sent to the court, or seat of government, of a foreign nation to transact diplomatic business.
notes: ☞ Ambassadors are classed (in the diplomatic sense) in the first rank of public ministers, ministers plenipotentiary in the second. “The United States diplomatic service employs two classes of ministers, -- ministers plenipotentiary and ministers resident.”

defs: One who serves at the altar; one who performs sacerdotal duties; the pastor of a church duly authorized or licensed to preach the gospel and administer the sacraments.

records.ety: [OE. ministren, OF. ministrer, fr. L. ministrare. See Minister, n.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Ministered (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Ministering.]

defs: To furnish or apply; to afford; to supply; to administer.
quotes: He that ministereth seed to the sower.
We minister to God reason to suspect us.

defs: To act as a servant, attendant, or agent; to attend and serve; to perform service in any office, sacred or secular.
quotes: The Son of man came not to be ministered unto, but to minister.

defs: To supply or to things needful; esp., to supply consolation or remedies; as, to minister to the sick.
quotes: Canst thou not minister to a mind diseased?
